concepts.name: Graph
concepts.short_definition: A collection of nodes (vertices) connected by edges.

concepts.name: Vertex
concepts.short_definition: A fundamental unit in a graph, representing an entity.

concepts.name: Edge
concepts.short_definition: A connection between two vertices in a graph.

concepts.name: Directed Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where edges have a direction, from one vertex to another.

concepts.name: Undirected Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where edges do not have a direction; connections are bidirectional.

concepts.name: Weighted Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where each edge has an associated numerical weight.

concepts.name: Unweighted Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where edges do not have associated weights.

concepts.name: Adjacency Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A 2D array representing graph connections, where rows and columns correspond to vertices.

concepts.name: Adjacency List
concepts.short_definition: A collection of lists, where each list contains the neighbors of a vertex.

concepts.name: Incidence Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A matrix where rows represent vertices and columns represent edges, indicating connections.

concepts.name: Sparse Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph with relatively few edges compared to the maximum possible number of edges.

concepts.name: Dense Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph with a large number of edges, close to the maximum possible.

concepts.name: Degree of a Vertex
concepts.short_definition: The number of edges incident to a vertex.

concepts.name: In-degree
concepts.short_definition: The number of edges directed into a vertex (in directed graphs).

concepts.name: Out-degree
concepts.short_definition: The number of edges directed out of a vertex (in directed graphs).

concepts.name: Neighbor
concepts.short_definition: A vertex directly connected to another vertex by an edge.

concepts.name: Graph Traversal
concepts.short_definition: Systematically visiting each vertex in a graph.

concepts.name: Breadth-First Search (BFS)
concepts.short_definition: A graph traversal algorithm that explores neighbors level by level.

concepts.name: Depth-First Search (DFS)
concepts.short_definition: A graph traversal algorithm that explores as far as possible along each branch before backtracking.

concepts.name: Queue
concepts.short_definition: A First-In, First-Out (FIFO) data structure.

concepts.name: Stack
concepts.short_definition: A Last-In, First-Out (LIFO) data structure.

concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.short_definition: The amount of memory an algorithm uses.

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: The amount of time an algorithm takes to run.

concepts.name: Array
concepts.short_definition: A contiguous block of memory storing elements of the same type.

concepts.name: Linked List
concepts.short_definition: A linear data structure where elements are linked using pointers.

concepts.name: Hash Table
concepts.short_definition: A data structure that implements an associative array, mapping keys to values.

concepts.name: Edge Case
concepts.short_definition: A specific scenario that might cause an algorithm to fail or behave unexpectedly.

concepts.name: Data Structure
concepts.short_definition: A particular way of organizing and storing data in a computer.

concepts.name: Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: A step-by-step procedure for solving a problem or accomplishing a task.

concepts.name: Big O Notation
concepts.short_definition: A mathematical notation used to describe the limiting behavior of a function.

concepts.name: Constant Time
concepts.short_definition: An operation that takes the same amount of time regardless of input size.

concepts.name: Linear Time
concepts.short_definition: An operation whose execution time grows linearly with the input size.

concepts.name: Quadratic Time
concepts.short_definition: An operation whose execution time grows with the square of the input size.

concepts.name: Logarithmic Time
concepts.short_definition: An operation whose execution time grows logarithmically with the input size.

concepts.name: Adjacency Matrix Space
concepts.short_definition: The space complexity of representing a graph using an adjacency matrix.

concepts.name: Adjacency List Space
concepts.short_definition: The space complexity of representing a graph using an adjacency list.

concepts.name: Adjacency Matrix Time
concepts.short_definition: The time complexity of operations on a graph represented by an adjacency matrix.

concepts.name: Adjacency List Time
concepts.short_definition: The time complexity of operations on a graph represented by an adjacency list.

concepts.name: Graph Representation Choice
concepts.short_definition: Selecting the appropriate method to represent a graph based on its properties and intended operations.
